findings.title: Unauthenticated GET endpoint: X-Admin-Key
findings.description:
The GET X-Admin-Key route has no visible authentication middleware. Sensitive operations should require authentication.
[AI Deep Verify: Attack scenario not viable — The code reads the X-Admin-Key header but there is no visible authentication middleware or validation logic. This means any request with any value in that header will be processed as if authenticated. This is a real missing authentication vulnerability.]

attack_scenario.path: 1. Attacker sends GET request to the admin endpoint with any X-Admin-Key header value. 2. The handler reads the header but does not validate it against a stored secret. 3. The handler proceeds to execute sensitive operations without authentication.
attack_scenario.impact: The attacker can perform any admin action without valid credentials, leading to full system compromise.
attack_scenario.explanation: The code reads the X-Admin-Key header but there is no visible authentication middleware or validation logic. This means any request with any value in that header will be processed as if authenticated. This is a real missing authentication vulnerability.

findings.title: Unauthenticated ANY endpoint: POST /api/admin/gen-codes
findings.description:
The ANY POST /api/admin/gen-codes route has no visible authentication middleware. Sensitive operations should require authentication.
[AI Deep Verify: Attack scenario not viable — The route is registered on adminMux but there is no authentication middleware applied. The handler will execute for any request, allowing an unauthenticated attacker to generate codes. This is a real missing authentication vulnerability.]

attack_scenario.path: 1. Attacker sends POST request to /api/admin/gen-codes. 2. The route is registered on adminMux without any authentication middleware. 3. The handler AdminGenCodes executes, generating redemption codes without verifying the caller's identity.
attack_scenario.impact: Attacker can generate unlimited valid redemption codes, leading to unauthorized access or resource abuse.
attack_scenario.explanation: The route is registered on adminMux but there is no authentication middleware applied. The handler will execute for any request, allowing an unauthenticated attacker to generate codes. This is a real missing authentication vulnerability.

findings.title: Potential JWT Secret Key
findings.description:
Regex pattern detected a potential JWT Secret Key at internal\handler\auth_test.go:22. This may be a hardcoded secret that automated scanners missed.
[AI Deep Verify: Attack scenario not viable — The secret is in a test file (auth_test.go) and is explicitly a test key. Test files are excluded from production builds. No real attack path exists.]

attack_scenario.explanation: The secret is in a test file (auth_test.go) and is explicitly a test key. Test files are excluded from production builds. No real attack path exists.

findings.title: Potential JWT Secret Key
findings.description:
Regex pattern detected a potential JWT Secret Key at internal\handler\auth_test.go:62. This may be a hardcoded secret that automated scanners missed.
[AI Deep Verify: Attack scenario not viable — Same as above – test file, test key. No real vulnerability.]

attack_scenario.explanation: Same as above – test file, test key. No real vulnerability.

findings.title: Potential Hex-encoded secret (32+ hex chars as string)
findings.description:
Regex pattern detected a potential Hex-encoded secret (32+ hex chars as string) at scripts\publish_telegraph.py:6. This may be a hardcoded secret that automated scanners missed.
[AI Deep Verify: Attack scenario not viable — The hex string is 47 characters long, which is not a standard key length (e.g., 32, 64). It is likely a placeholder or example token. The file is in a scripts directory, not production code. No evidence it is used in a security context.]

attack_scenario.explanation: The hex string is 47 characters long, which is not a standard key length (e.g., 32, 64). It is likely a placeholder or example token. The file is in a scripts directory, not production code. No evidence it is used in a security context.

findings.description: Regex pattern detected a potential AWS account ID (12 digits) at internal\handler\auth_test.go:22. This may be a hardcoded secret that automated scanners missed.

findings.description: Regex pattern detected a potential AWS account ID (12 digits) at internal\handler\auth_test.go:62. This may be a hardcoded secret that automated scanners missed.

findings.title: SBOM generated: 89 components detected
findings.description: Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) generated in CycloneDX format. 89 components inventoried for vulnerability tracking.

findings.title: AUTOINCREMENT without protection in redeem_codes.sql (x1 occurrences)
findings.description: Autoincrement IDs can leak information (order of creation, total count). Found at lines: 2
findings.fix_suggestion: Consider using UUID or ULID for primary keys in user-facing tables.

findings.title: AUTOINCREMENT without protection in schema.sql (x11 occurrences)
findings.description: Autoincrement IDs can leak information (order of creation, total count). Found at lines: 6, 21, 38, 50, 57, 67, 78, 88, 97, 104, 126
findings.fix_suggestion: Consider using UUID or ULID for primary keys in user-facing tables.

findings.title: SBOM generation available via syft
findings.description: Run 'syft scan <target> -o cyclonedx-json' to generate a Software Bill of Materials. SBOMs are required for supply chain transparency (US Executive Order 14028).

findings.title: 10/10 recent commits are not GPG signed
findings.description: Unsigned commits can be forged by attackers who gain access to git configuration. GPG-sign all commits to establish cryptographic proof of authorship.
findings.fix_suggestion: Configure git GPG signing: git config --global commit.gpgsign true

findings.title: OpenSSF Scorecard not installed
findings.description: Install OpenSSF Scorecard (https://github.com/ossf/scorecard) to get automated security health checks for your dependencies and build pipeline.
